title: chrysothamnus nauseosus subsp. bernardinus
intro: Chrysothamnus nauseosus subsp. bernardinus, also known as Big Sagebrush, is a shrub native to the Great Basin region in North America, characterized by its greyish-green leaves and clusters of small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Big Sagebrush have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. It can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to digestive problems.
